analysis.name: Adam Bowers

analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 275 reviews — this is a reliable picture

review_highlights.comment: Yo I don't even need to say much. Can't describe in words besides "if professor Judy Kim was the worst, HE IS THE BEST PROFESSOR IN THE UNIVERSE". Wow, he's such a nice person, he posts all his lecture notes in the website. His notes very understandable. He has that skill to summarize all the complicated math concepts in textbook in his own words concisely so that students get easy time understanding the whole concepts. Exams? Don't worry. As long as you know the basic concepts, you'll pass. Oh

Bowers does not care for your well being at all. I've never been so destroyed by any class in my life. I look around at the faces of the students around me and I can see we are all under a common suffering that bonds us. If there is punishment in the afterlife, it is taking this class for eternity. The midterms hurt more than a broken heart.

Bowers has easy lectures, but his actual exams and homework are much harder than his lecture content. This is because he explains the bare minimum of each concept in class and expects us to show full concept mastery at a high level on the test. Like training kids to walk a 12 minute mile and then making them run a marathon for the final.

The examples he uses in his lectures are like 1+12. However, when it was time to do the homework or the exam, it was totally different than what was expected. I thought I was the only one struggling in the class, but I noticed others around me were too. "We're all in this together..." 50% average for passing on midterms and no curve...
he is the best but the  final was too dificult

analysis.name: Jon Aycock
analysis.verdict: Most students had a tough time — consider alternatives if possible. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 65 reviews — fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment: Every single lecture thought nothing.Went too slow at the start and gave no examples then went really fast and didn't explain. Practice exams are nothing like the test, and the tests are nothing like the examples in lectures or textbooks. If you are taking him, read the textbook all by yourself, and even then you might get caught off-guard on tests

My whole friend group failed the class. We got the average grade above on both midterms since we focused on study guides and practice problems. When we got to the final we had the same strategy and got a completely different test that was never on the homework, practice problems, or any past midterms (with the exception of 2 questions).NEVER AGAIN.

professor did not give any relevant review material, always stated that we should "use the textbook & hw problems" as practice, yet the exam was nothing like the questions that we did on the homework. rushed during lecture only to get nowhere and put concepts he covered the lecture before the midterm (didn't even complete the topic) on the midterm

Never again. His policies do not help you succeed at all and on top of that everything is messy and unorganized with most of his sources being ripped from other teachers. i literally saw another midterm for a diff teacher and it was exactly like our practice exam. he makes the exams 10x harder and makes u be prepared for other teachers exams

No cheatsheets, no curve, no calculators. Lectures are confusing, talks about theorems all the time and barely gave examples. Talks about 20A and 20B before gets to 20C and runs out of time. Skips parts of sections, ended up learning almost everything by myself. Exams were difficult, practice exams has nothing to do with the actual one.

analysis.name: Anne Yilmaz
analysis.verdict: Below average reviews — check if this fits your learning style
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 32 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: Professor Yilmaz clearly knows the subject (she keeps reminding you of her expert status). But often questions felt like they were met with annoyance or sarcasm, so it was hard to feel comfortable asking for help. It honestly felt like the class was harder than it needed to be because of the way she communicates. The slides were well made though.

Interesting topic but the class is unnecessarily difficult. Exams are extremely concept-based and require very precise answers that often feel trickier than what is emphasized in lecture. She talks a lot about her own research and can come across as annoyed when students ask questions, which makes the class feel intimidating. Avoid the class.

Exams don't correspond with lecture material. They are significantly more difficult and feel as if she did not teach what appears on exams. Along with her condescending attitude toward students' questions, it feels intentional, as if she does not want students to succeed. The most unrelated exam questions to lectures I have experienced.

I do not recommend taking her class. She goes on personal tangents about her career and "expertise" in eye witness testimony. The textbook readings do not align with the lectures that are for that particular week. Her exams are confusing and not based on lectures or readings. She is unable to answer student questions on material presented.

Avoid this class, this class is heavy on material. TA's didn't know much of the material, student's would ask questions and would often say "I'm not sure" or would look it up in their computer. Honestly if you miss points for HW your grade goes down heavily. I asked professor for help and she direct me to the TA's which didn't help.

analysis.name: Andrew Barton
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews — experiences vary a lot between students. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 13 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: Super chill class and very nice professor. Memory based so I used flash cards to study. Practice questions very similar to exams, and he did have some trick questions on exams but in my opinion they were easy to catch. Discussions had a decent amount of work to do, and attendance for both lecture and discussion was required. Pretty easy A

3 tests and a final. Attendance is mandatory, but he talks extremely fast, so you will barely hear everything. Uses clickers for attendance, which is 10% of the grade. Tests are completely made up of trick questions. If only he had straightforwardly asked questions, a lot more students would have succeeded.

Taught half the class, had really interesting lectures, and explained content well. Lecture and discussion were mandatory and provided good study material. No trick questions on exams but definitely still required a decent amount of studying, and you can drop 1 of the 3 midterms or the final. I found lectures very engaging and loved the topics.

This is one of the best professors I've had. His lectures are super organized and easy to follow. He lays out very clearly what will be on exams and gives in-class practice questions that are extremely similar to the exam questions. You will still need to study & memorize but an A is super achievable.
Exams were overall representative of what was taught in the lecture. The material was often confusing, however, he was always open to answering questions from students. Attendance is mandatory.

analysis.name: Leesa Anzaldo
analysis.verdict: Solid pick — well-liked with reasonable difficulty
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 113 reviews — this is a reliable picture

review_highlights.comment: Her lectures are based textbook, there are a lot of theorems but she explains them, and she is willing to answer questions about the theorems and how they work. She goes through practice problems too. Tests have true/false section about theorems and a practical section where you apply equations. Homework helps you practice, BUT it costs $100.

10/10 professor. Exams are very similar to practice tests. Final is "cumulative" but more than 3/4 is represented of post midterm 2. HW is guaranteed 100%, infinite tries. 6/9 discussions are mandatory for full credit. Study discussions. Watch 3Blue1Brown linear algebra. PLEASE read the textbook and do the problems. Watch out for polynomials!

Honestly, she is an amazing professor. She teaches through examples, which was a much more effective approach (for me). She listens to the topics that students struggle with and prepare questions for the entire class to do together. Her tests reflect on the topics covered in class, and grading is fair.

She is genuinely one of the best teachers you will ever have! She's super kind, genuine, and helps you prepare for tests very well. All you have to do is attend class, do the practice exams, and put time into the homeworks and you will be good. Definitely do the discussion worksheets (will help you a LOT).

She's a very nice and respectable professor with lectures that are SEMI-mandatory (You can miss 6-7) and same with discussions (You can also miss 2-3) and still receive full credit for attendance. Grading wise, very easy on everything BUT midterms and finals as they weigh about 80% of your grade. Stay caught up and this class will be light :P

analysis.name: Andrew  Pun
analysis.verdict: Good teacher but expect a challenge
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 23 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: I like how he uses the whiteboard since it shows everyone a slower process step by step and I appreciated that. Overall a good teacher, he wants to see his students improve. Tests were representative of what he lectured, as well as discussion section hw, and the lecture slides he provides.

Ochem is hard regardless, even attending lectures I was so confused I feel as though he is too smart to be able to dumb it down more. I personally did good in ochem I and was not expecting to feel so lost and behind in ochem II. The format of exams does not allow for partial credit since only the answer is graded, which makes it very difficult too!

Actually goated. I loved the way he taught the class. He made ochem enjoyable and I loved learning it! I did avg (70%s) on the midterms but lowkey popped off on the final and got an A-. Idk if he curved but I'm assuming he did, although the curve wouldn't be much because all his tests were mc. His tests were a fair representation of the content.

Exams were multiple choice which is kinda strange for an ochem class. Because of this i've tanked points for small errors, but averages were 70 on both midterms. No curve since averages were high. He does a good job explaining things, but building intuition for mechanisms is challenging regardless. Spam practice problems and ur probably fine

difficult class but you can succeed if you really put the work in. i got a 70% on the first exam but got 100% on the second and clutched up the final and got an A in the class. good lectures, he chalks and explains each step, often giving us time to do the problems ourself before tackling it on the board. good at explaining concepts.

analysis.name: Achinta Nandi
analysis.verdict: Below average reviews — check if this fits your learning style
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 14 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: He skips lectures without prior announcement. He also says he'll post the lectures he missed but never posts them. The midterm also had two whole problems with wrong numbers that left me confused.

The exams were not particularly difficult and were mostly based on the material we had learned in class. There were also bonus points available on the test. The professor is very sociable and clearly very knowledgeable. Attendance is not required for in-person lectures, as all classes are recorded and made available for later viewing.

Often went on tangents during lecture, commonly moved class to online with short notice or canceled class, saying there would be a posted lecture but it never happened. Handwriting is not legible, only writes on chalkboard. Homework was never graded in a timely manner and midterm reviews had no posted keys. Overall grade curve was generous

Its a proofs class. You pretty much need to understand everything. It gets hard around chapter 14 or week 7. Has a bad time with releasing recorded lectures on time and has a hard time explaining harder subjects. Overall, would still take if doing pass no pass, but not sure about letter grade. I believe he will curve though

The professor cares about the student's understanding when he explains things in class and explains concepts/train of thought mostly clearly. Sometimes goes on side tangents which are unrelated to course material, and occasionally makes mistakes on the board. Also, as one review said, he promises video lectures but ends up not recording them.

analysis.name: Alex Chaim
analysis.verdict: Solid pick — well-liked with reasonable difficulty. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 6 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Chaim's lectures were really helpful and he made it easy to understand the content. The exams also had buffer questions which made it easier to get an A even if you didn't know every little detail. The class is memorization heavy in general, but Chaim and the IA's made it easier :)
Professor is clear about what will be tested. Participation points can be gained in discussion section. All tests have buffer questions, which makes the exam and studying less stressful.
One of the most compassionate professors I've had at UCSD. He genuinely wants to see you succeed and will do what he can to support you when things get tough.10/10 would take again

Very clear lecturer, explains what parts of mechanisms you need to know, and problem sets are a very clear reflection of the exams. Class is entirely memorization but exams have buffer questions so getting answers wrong can still get you an A. 10% of grade was participation in class or through video recordings, but pretty easy to earn. A nice prof!

The class was distributed between 2 midterms, a final, and 10 part points. 
It was his first time teaching the course and I felt he did amazing, was clear during lectures, often ended early (huge plus)clear study guide, midterms were reflective of the material taught. 
The final was a little tougher, but if you do the study guides you will be fine

analysis.name: Amna Salam
analysis.verdict: Solid pick — well-liked with reasonable difficulty
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 5 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: for 104V, love her, she's very cool. Easy hw assignments, the midterm and final were relatively easy because of the study guides she provided. I didn't read the textbook because everything we needed to know was on her slides, she made that clear from day 1. I'm not a fan of group presentations but overall the class was easy, I also learned a lot!

Little to no homework (I spent ten hours or less of working outside of lecture), which is a lot less than my other poli sci classes), just doing 3 memos and preparing for one group presentation. Midterm and final are straightforward and easy to study for. The lectures could be more interesting though. Her outfits eat and she is a cool person!

No podcasts but slides are fairly one to one with what she is saying if you miss lecture. Graded by 3 assignments, midterm, and final. Provides feedback and help if you're ever confused on the material. She also made some lectures very interactive and fun, playing little games that coincide with what we're learning for the week.

Class is straight to the point. Grade consists of three memos, group presentations and closed book midterm/final. Downside; rude and has yelled at the class for talking before a presentation, but not many people talking. Could've approached it differently. Regardless, if you're ok with group presentations & closed book exams, take her, its doable.

I really like her; she is like a diva. Her tests are just like the study guide; you would end up with an A if you study it. Group presentations are not as bad as people make it seem. she is actually nice yes she yelled at the class but for a valid reason no one likes to be talked over.

analysis.name: Kim Albizati
analysis.verdict: Good teacher but expect a challenge
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 231 reviews — this is a reliable picture

review_highlights.comment: He's highly overrated.  He's somewhat approachable, you just have to remember that he wont answer questions during class time but you can see him after class.  Doesn't really explain mechanisms in detail, teaches it more like a Biology class with memorizing reactions and a general mechanism.  This makes it very hard to understand what's going on.

He is all hype; his curve makes it impossible to get an A (you need to be at least 80 points above average) and he never reveals his grading criteria (his TAs don't know either). Didn't show up for the final review session or the final exam. Exam averages are pretty low but he makes the exams consistently harder. Tells you to try every book problem

hardest curve of all the ochem profs (need at least 80 points above average for an A), very secretive about grading criteria. Gives very few practice problems, no extra credit, no HW, so grade based entirely on exams. Tells you to try every book problem but they are unrelated and not helpful for his class since his question style is so different.

Here are the hard facts: his curve is EXTREMELY difficult; you need to get at least 80 points above average in order to get an A. Never posts grades until AFTER the final so you are left wondering all quarter. Grade based entirely on exams (which are not easy). He puts challenge questions on exams over topics he never covered in class. Overrated.

I think Albizati is kinda overrated. I mean he is funny but sometimes he skips over basic knowledge but expects you to know how to do it on the homework. The material that he does teach is taught very well though. The TAs' quizzes were the hardest part of the class. Albizati doesn't have the best office hours nor does he extend them for finals.

analysis.name: Brian Leigh
analysis.verdict: Good teacher but expect a challenge. But trending upward recently

review_highlights.comment: Prof Leigh starts every lecture with some chemistry memes pertaining to the material of the day and throws in hilarious quips and puns to keep the class interested. He's very knowledgeable and gives in-depth examples of lab calculations on the class website. Take his class!

Hilarious to listen to + cares for students; bit unclear what is wanted in reports at times; lectures not mandatory + flexible.

Midterm good; barely prepped us for the final so I had to memorize everything and still felt uneasy.

Do not take with other time-consuming classes. Despite going to TA helproom every day, I still got tons of points off.

Very nice and funny professor. 
He tries very hard to curve at the end. 
Lab reports are graded very detailed. Sigfigs can cost you many points. 
He posted videos about past midterms and the midterms questions are exactly like those in the video. 
Final exam pretty hard, full of lecture and detailed experimental questions. 
Go to office hours.

I should have been warned about this class a little more and my grade would have been way better. GO TO HIS LECTURES. The sig fig grading is so insane (double check graphs, data, calculations, and write ups). The final is evil but he does curve. Is very witty and wants everyone to succeed. This class requires a lot of effort so keep that in mind.

Chem 7L is a beast especially because prelabs and lab reports are so time consuming, but the labs themselves were very fun and Prof. Leigh made it so much better. He is super funny and really cares about his students. His lectures are very through on how to complete each experiment and even gives tips on how to not get dinged on the lab report!

analysis.name: Benjamin Ochoa

Like sending you to Academic Integrity Office, where they said my teammate did not violate any rule. He keeps being harsh on my teammate and changed our final project language from python to C++......

Worst class I've taken at UCSD. Homework and exams barely relate to the lectures. The instructor gets irritated easily, even when students ask normal questions, and often argues instead of explaining. Very disrespectful attitude overall. Avoid this class if possible — truly exhausting experience.

Ochoa isn't the best lecturer, but provides the info you need through pre-class vids. The material requires a lot of self-teaching, there's no way around it. It was my first coding class, but I was pretty dedicated to learning the material. With time and practice on PAs, you'll be fine. The exams were extremely easy compared to PA's.4% EC offered.

Definitely don't take this class if you have a lot on your plate already- it's a lot of time commitment and SO NOT worth it for the basic level of java I know now. He's a nice guy and whatever, but HOLYY his class destroyed me. I spent like 70% of my time this quarter doing the homework. You'll either hate or love cse11, and a lot of ppl hated it.

He may be bad at teaching, he may be giving a lot of homework, he may enjoy making easy things redundant, but he somehow still has a job at UCSD. This shows that the job market is not as bleak as it seems if someone like Ochoa has held a job at UCSD for years.

analysis.name: Zachary Brants
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews — experiences vary a lot between students. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 20 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: I truly believe Brants is a solid professor. He never fails to be energetic and shows great passion for philosophy. His lectures were fun at the start though it got a little dull near the end. 5 P/NP assignments for the whole quarter. Study guide is provided, easy A for the midterm. Pop quizzes are P/NP and quite easy even w/o doing the reading.

Professor Brant's is an excellent lecturer who makes the content very interesting. The reviews who rate him poorly do not reflect his teaching style. If you do the readings (which are quite lengthy but interesting) you will be fine on pop quizzes. If you are looking for a throw away class and dont care to learn then dont take the course.

I thought this professor was excellent. He was very descriptive in his slides. Gave study guides for exams and easy pop quizzes on readings. If you participate in class discussions you will definitely learn more. Participating in class is a key part of your education so don't listen to the bad reviews it's people who don't do the work.

His passion for philosophy is admirable, but a little too much, as he often goes over time during lectures. Attendance is not considered mandatory, but he does do pop quizzes to ensure students are attending class. The lectures just go over the readings, and they're not engaging. Also, he has a no-technology policy, so come to class with a notebook

The professor is a great, passionate lecturer, and does a good job engaging the class in discussion. There's no attendance, but there are pop quizzes, which are easy to pass if you did the reading, which is interesting and not long. The essays are pass/fail and you get much leeway on how to write them. Midterms and finals are easy.

analysis.name: Brian Shotwell
analysis.verdict: Below average reviews — check if this fits your learning style
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 215 reviews — this is a reliable picture

review_highlights.comment: Shotwell does not know how to teach at all. Attendance is mandatory (3 lecture classes plus mandatory discussion and quiz sections EVERY WEEK). Assigns 20+ homework problems every week and quizzes are unnecessarily hard. Also has pre lecture quizzes based off videos. People who like him are either teachers' pets or nerds. Do not recommend at all.

I was part of the 40% that failed. I just spend 1,500 in tuition at ucsd to retake the course with the regrade policy. I could be hawaii. He's tests are ridiculous and none of the questions are on ANY resources he provides. Just a wait a quarter I promise i'm trying to help. I should be tanning in hawaii rn. I wish him nothing but misery.

Yes, if you self-study a lot, you can get an A but that's entirely because you're teaching yourself. His lectures don't clarify much, and he shows little interest in actually helping students learn. So yes, it's possible to do well but only by ignoring his teaching altogether. You'll succeed in spite of him, not because of him.

"[People] really are treating you like you're dumb. And I'm the only one not treating you like you're dumb. So it feels bad that you're not doing well on exams, but really, I know that you can do better," said the prof, who consistently implies that the SP25 Phys 2A class failed bc they didn't try hard enough.

It's really not worth your money to take this class. In terms of learning, grades, and time spent, it just doesn't pay off. Take it with someone else instead, it's worth waiting a quarter if you have to.

analysis.name: Bennett Chow

review_highlights.comment: Lectures: Often very unorganized, although informative if able to follow along. HW: (20%) Followed mainly from book, and some from lectures. DO NOT rec prof for final alone!!!!!!!!
Chow gives pretty clear exam range. Unlike other professors, chow focuses more on proving and understanding concepts. Hes always passionate during class. Great overall!

So by past finals he posted and the midterm, I expected that most of the problems would be similar to the homework and lecture problems with a few of them being extra difficult.  I'm not sure what happened this time around but the final was ridiculously difficult with only a few similar to class problems. Prof did not prepare us for final at all...

Ben Chow isn't a good professor by any means for math 109, he does have practice exams available but those only help so much. In class he often dwelled on very conceptual proofs which he got lost in. The class introduces a new way of thinking mathematically but Chow does a poor job of delivering these important concepts. A nice guy, but stay away.
One of the few math professors that makes my understand what he is talking about. Lectures gives a thorough understand of the concepts and exams are mostly discussed problems.

analysis.name: Mary Boyle
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews — experiences vary a lot between students

review_highlights.comment: She is very unreasonable and tries her best to seem like a good person, but likes to give trick questions and mislead students on the quizzes and midterms she has. The only good thing about the class are the ta's who actually provide you with information about the quizzes, which the lectures and reading do almost  nothing to prepare you for.

A huge amount of time in lecture was lost to her small talk, together with her playing with or disciplining her untrained, distracting dog, and singling out students for questions (to which she often admits she cannot expect us to know the answer). When she's singling out students in class, she will ask them loaded questions about her teaching.

1% extra credit for four (4) SONA hrs, 1% for several hundred pages of reading + quizzes, 2% total. The reading quizzes are separate from the discussion quizzes, and you're quizzed before you even discuss the material. Many questions on midterms and quizzes are misleading, very similar, or taken from very specific passages. AVOID her.

Great personality, terrible educator. Lecture is messy and filled with unhelpful stories of her personal life. Occasionally, she will make a comment on what exactly she expects us to know for the midterms. These comments are the only reason to attend lecture; otherwise, lecture is a complete waste of time.

Extremely rude and insensitive. Worst professor I've had. She expects students to know the answer even though this course material is very new to a lot of these students. She is very unclear and vague with her questions. She gets irritated when you don't answer correctly, even though she says "just try answering, it's ok if you get it wrong."

analysis.name: Jor-El Briones

His homework quiz policy got me crazy. It's really discouraging that your final grade can be dragged down by a single quiz question, no matter how hard you work on the other exams.Don't take his Math18  unless you're prepared to deal with a huge amount of stress.

as specific as the other reviews are, nothing can perfectly articulate how hellish this class was. the amount of work and stress I had in this class was ridiculous. he treated the class like we were curing cancer even though this math isn't even for math related majors. I SUFFERED SO YOU DON'T HAVE TO!!!! PLEASE FOR THE LOVE OF GOD DON'T TAKE HIM!!

Mandatory disccusions w/ quizzes and have to get 100% to get credit. If you have 99% of the problem correct, he deducts 5% off your total final grade. Deducts 2% from final grade for each discussion you miss. You could get 100% on his midterms and final exam and still fail the class in theory. His grading style does not want you to pass the class.

analysis.name: Brian Zid
analysis.verdict: Good teacher but expect a challenge. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 120 reviews — this is a reliable picture

review_highlights.comment: Awesome chem prof. chem 6a does have a lot of content, can be hard but he is really helpful during office hours, lectures where useful but confusing at times on certain topics that took me a sec to learn, just do the practice reviews and GO TO DISCUSSION, its not required but no one goes and I treated it as a tutoring session which helped me a lot.

Zid is amazing. He tells you exactly what is going to be on each midterm and the final, and tells you exactly what to study. People who got a bad grade just didn't go to the lecture. Go to the lecture and follow along, and this will be a very good Chem 6A class. The discussion worksheets are also very good and actually helpful. Lecture podcasted.

Brian is, overall, a good professor, but sometimes he explains things in an overcomplicated way or out of order. His lectures are podcasted, and attendance isn't mandatory for the lecture or section (though you do have to do a discussion sheet graded on completion). He gives a practice midterm for the first midterm, but not the second one.

Professor Zid was great!! Very clear slides that make the material pretty simple to understand. Sometimes he goes a bit fast. Two Midterms that are both fairly easy if you study a decent amount. There are no surprise questions, he tells you pretty much everything that will be on the exams. Discussion sections and lectures are not mandatory; podcast

Never took chem so I struggled but he was so kind. He said it can be a hard class if you have 0 history of chem. I struggled but SI sessions helped me alot. His lectures are very reflective of his exams, no cheat sheet, no extra credit. HW was a frustrating software, my O-Chem friends struggled with it. Would rec taking chem 4 first if no chem hx.

analysis.name: Hsiao-Bing Cheng
analysis.verdict: Most students had a tough time — consider alternatives if possible. And trending downward

review_highlights.comment: Unless your only goal is to become more proficient in your prayer life, do not take this class!!! If you see his name on webreg, waitlist another professor instead and if you already have him, just drop the class. He told us to drop since Day 1 and I wish I listened. Take literally any other professor, his class is incredibly unfair in comparison.

Self study class mostly no clear outline. No cheat sheets no study guides so it'll be more difficult. Tests are mostly based on homework but require a deeper understanding of the material to be applied to similar problems. Exam averages in 40s-50s while other profs are 80s. Not a big deal since there will be a big curve later on.

He constantly makes mistakes like forgetting minus signs, denominators, and forgets to add all relevant information needed to solve a problem. The hw was not as hard as the exams but takes 6+ hours a week. He assigned quizzes (2 hours each) on the same week as midterms. 0/10 Professor. He said 63% (F) was a very good score for our first midterm.

Very frustrating in every aspect. 10+ hours being spent on homework per week and I don't even get full credit most of the time. He does a very bad job preparing us for homework, quizzes, and tests. You HAVE to teach yourself everything. His lectures are a mess....just spare yourself from the headaches and take this course elsewhere.

He goes though lectures very quickly and expects you to absorb everything the minute he says something. He will do 1-2 examples on the topic but then the homework and test will have complex variations that you barely know how to do. I can see that he likes his job and knows the content, but he is not that good at explaining things well or in depth.

analysis.name: Lisa Cartwright
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews — experiences vary a lot between students

Taking him rn, and it's actually insane. Midterms are 60 MC and his lectures are all over the place. He talks very fast and he kind of sidetracks the material. analysis.name: Priyanga Ganesan

Homework cannot be completed just by knowledge from lectures. Midterms are hard and contains some grand new questions. Final weighs 40 to 50 percent.

Her midterms are not doable, and little curve was applied to adjust the grades. Taking her class puts you disadvantaged since even if you know the material, she makes it exponentially harder than it should be (as shown through comparison with other sections) to bring your grades down.

Took Math 18 with her but it was a collaborative course with another professor who had better notes and similar exams. Exams that she made were hard compared to other sections, not a lot of people finished. Do not take her for a class if she is the only one teaching it.

Dr. Ganesan isn't the most engaging lecturer but her expectations are clear and tests are fair/easy to prep for. Though I attended most of the lectures, I learned much more from the textbook and the Engineering Learning Community tutoring (shout out to Nathan!).

Once you figure out how to best approach the material, the course is a breeze.

analysis.name: Ivonne Gonzalez Gamboa

Professor Gamboa is so sweet and cares a lot about her students. She makes sure to clearly go over all of the conceptual material necessary to understand the lab for the week. analysis.name: Clark Gibson

reading assignments due before class and weekly team quizzes (open note). harsh grader on midterm/final: all writing on blue book, 3 short answers (2 paragraphs) and one long essay (4-5 paragraphs), cheat sheet allowed. grades weren't posted till week 7 unfortunately.

analysis.name: Mathew Holloway

analysis.name: Jonathan Ruiz

Easy to understand, genuinely a good class if you want a grade-booster. Discussion attendance opts as your lecture attendance so show up to those.

analysis.name: Jack Wesley

Young af, kind professor. Calc 2 is hard though and since he isn't an experienced professor and is teaching such a hard course it is pretty difficult. analysis.name: Kathy Lacefield

Quizzes were your entire grade, but they were fair and she drops the lowest score. If you read the book, you can succeed even without paying attention in lecture.

Attendance not mandatory. Don't recommend if Thomas Morton is the TA. Exams are extremely specific. Allowed cheat sheets every exam. No required textbook. Professor is nice but mocks people with substance use disorders

analysis.name: Ryan Kastner

analysis.name: Kyle Kehrer

his class might be a little boring, but i think every physics class is just boring in its own way. His grading scale is very nice and the quizzes are very similar to the final. 4 quizzes, 1 final, lowerst can count lower if ur final is higher than it. overall pretty good

analysis.name: Kenneth Zeger

Good news is that most people don't self study, but if you do, you will be far ahead of the game.

analysis.name: Kristina Markman

analysis.name: Maysa Nichter

analysis.name: Richard Norris

Bach taught the material well, but his exam questions felt like they were intentionally trying to trick you. When I took this course in fall, it was combined with the other MATH18 classes. I don't know about the other professors, but I liked Anzaldo's lectures better. If you have the opportunity, take her.

If you want to destroy your GPA, take his class. No guidance whatsoever on the exams. You just have to memorize everything from the textbook or squeeze them all in your cheat sheet. His lecture notes are filled with errors. Also, questions on exams are totally different from what we learnt so studying is useless because you're gonna fail anyway.

The professor teaches well if you actually pay attention but his class is a lot of work. We had HW due every week with 20 questions for each HW and no the questions weren't easy. Exams are tough but grading is a bit lenient. Overall, I'd say he's a tough professor since he could make the class easier with less HW and assignments but oh well.

analysis.name: Qin Hong Anderson
analysis.verdict: Great professor — tough but students love the teaching. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 63 reviews — fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment: Prof. Anderson is super sweet and funny! She's also very good at explaining grammar patterns and Chinese cultural tidbits. There are mandatory video projects, but you have the option to work alone.

Qin Laoshi is an amazing professor. She is an expert on the subject, and makes learning Chinese fun and enjoyable. She is kind and respectful, but also expects her students to perform well. This combination makes for a really rewarding experience. Out of all of the Chinese professors I have ever had, Qin Laoshi is the best. I HIGHLY recommend her.

Qin laoshi is an excellent professor.  A lot of 2nd year students are taking Chinese for fun, not because they have to, and Qin sees that and tries to keep everybody learning in an environment that is fun and engaging.  Don't doze off in class though; she will regularly call on everybody in the room!

If you got by speaking English in your 20 series classes, be sure you practice speaking Chinese to prepare for class discussions (entirely in Chinese). Participation is really key so that you get the most out of the lessons. Schedule to meet with Qin laoshi if you need help. She's really passionate about her students.

Best Chinese teacher I have ever had in my life. I took Chinese out of interest and not for credit. She truly cares about her students' learning and she tries her best to support you if you are confused or are falling behind. Her lectures are funny and engaging; very detailed and easy to understand. She makes all her students love the language.

analysis.name: Quazi Shahriar

review_highlights.comment: lectures are him just reading off of the slides that are posted on canvas but has iclicker and makes class mandatory. tests seem super easy when taking them but then i did so bad every time, and each one is worth more than 20% of your final grade so if you aren't a good test taker, i don't recommend this prof.

He posts terrible slides with fill-in-the-blanks that are only convenient for taking notes if you print out every slide before class. I took ECON 487 in the fall 2017 semester. I highly recommend getting the course reader that has plenty of examples. I used the textbook for its exercises which paid off. The quizzes and exams are definitly fair & are similiar to the examples that are discussed in class and in the textbook. He was easy to listen to, very collected and motivated!

analysis.name: Vu Quang Nguyen

omg this guy is insane to understand and he really doesn't go in depth in topics at all unless someone asks him a question. go to ur discussions and ur ta's will be extremely helpful. nothing about the homework is explicitly covered in class and he really is all over the place. he says "um" and "uhh" like 10000 times during lecture. sweet guy tho.

no clear grading criteria on problem sets, doing homework on mcgrawhill was extremely frustrating to deal with. course overall is disorganized and prof doesn't explain material clearly, lecture material has little to no correlation with homework problems. please wait another quarter for another genetics prof if possible

Professor Reyes is a funny, caring, and attentive professor. Her lectures are easy to follow and her teaching style is consistent, content is also very consistent with homework. analysis.name: Raymond Chou

This man might actually be one of the greatest professors of all time. I will truly miss his enthusiasm and humor. He is the John Keating of the modern day. I have such high respect for this man. Professor Raymond is sincerely very passionate and engaging with the material. He cares about you understanding the material, and his lectures are very dynamic and interesting. Can't recommend him enough. He's the GOAT.

Lectures are very fun, enjoyable, and covers a lot of what would be on the exams. Cheerful and passionate professor. Lectures moved a bit fast and the content is difficult if you go in without an immense love for math. I thought he was funny! Passionate about what he teaches. Has accent, but very understandable. 

Lectures/readings way harder than tests. Posts most L. notes, kind of hard to follow. 

Only midterm 45% and Final 55%. Gives ALL TEST QUESTIONS class before. Closed note, No EC, tweaks only for low grades. 4/5 for incongruence between lectures/tests.

Easy class as long as you put in the work, ~3/4 hrs a week for hw. Weekly quizzes at beginning of class, super easy if u do the readings, lowest score is dropped. Study guides match exams. R is challenging but doable, stats section is easy. As long as you pay attention in class (my rec is to always bring snacks) and do the readings, you'll do well.

Lectures in class were long and detailed, but everything was explained fairly well. In class quizzes every week but as long as you study for midterm/final your grade will def be fine

pretty bad. all good ratings you see came from when the quizzes and exams were taken online.

This was the first time the professor taught this class in Julia so he wrote a lot of math on the whiteboards to explain what the coding was doing instead of having prepared slides so I got lost sometimes but he was very kind and patient and answered questions during and after class. Great professor, don't know what's with the bad reviews.

his tests were easy, but very monotone during lecture, to me he seems like he'd rather be doing research full time than teaching. often ended lectures early but would have to send out emails re-explaining things he did not properly go over, i would rather have the full lecture time be used so students get correct lecture info

This professor couldn't make up his mind on what should count for attendance, moved half the class to online zoom, his entire canvas page is run through just the "files" tab (no modules), and during the first midterm it took him like 25 minutes to pass out exams. analysis.name: Uri Gneezy
analysis.verdict: Below average reviews — check if this fits your learning style. And trending downward

The strat that helped me get an A is just to write 150-200 words on summarizing the vids and the other 200 words on the lesson. Make sure to explicitly say "the lesson i take away is..." or something like that. Super easy class.

The class is run exclusively by TAs it would seem, Gneezy could not even be bothered to send a single announcement himself. The percentile grading sucks, you won't get a grade based on your effort, which is ridiculous given that Gneezy doesn't appear to put in much effort himself. Very few assignments, if you miss even one you're cooked.

Honestly the whole class was decently easy, although I find it ridiculous that he uses AI in grading as part of his course. On top of having to pay for a reskinned canvas. I received lower grades on average when graded by AI and it pushed my grade down. The final touch was that grades are based on percentile, so I went from a 93 to a B+.
Super easy class that takes about an hour a week. I felt that the professor did not have much of an impact on my learning in this course, but he made the course very easy.

I would 100% take this prof again. She's the goat of warren writing. It's not like this class is SUPER easy or anything but if you have to pick a professor theres no reason not to pick Emma.

Cares a lot about her students and gives great feedback on essays. Grading contract makes it nearly impossible to get a lower grade than you want. Would take this professor again for every WCWP course if I could.

Practice tests are very reflective of exams, just uses different numbers. Probably one of the easier 20D professors by far.

The midterms and final were pretty fair based on the lecture content and given practice exams. I honestly stopped showing up to the lectures after the first midterm because it felt like he went over the content too fast. Homework problems were okay and the lowest HW grade gets dropped.

Super fun lecture and professor, midterm and essays had note sheets, we do read a lot though. Easy grader fun topics, computers allowed.

analysis.name: Siddharth Vishwanath
analysis.verdict: Below average reviews — check if this fits your learning style
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 32 reviews — fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment: negative comments are too dramatic. sure, sometimes it was hard to follow lecture because some concepts are over complicated and convention is changed. homeworks were long and sometimes difficult too. but exams were fair and thats the most important part since its a large chunk of grade. attendance technically not mandatory but we have pop quizzes.

Furthermore, you can't even argue to justify a convention because they NEVER review your regrade requests.

He rushed through the last few weeks; the textbook's bad, and he's teaching us how to merely solve a singular type of problem, not the concept itself.

He is approachable and supportive. He gives extra credit for answering in class, and per the Fall 2024 syllabus, the final can replace your midterm grade, which really helps. He explains concepts clearly and is fair with grading. Sometimes he arrives a little late, but it's rare and understandable. If you attend lectures and study, you'll succeed.

analysis.verdict: Below average reviews — check if this fits your learning style
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 29 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: She is a brilliant professor - knows her stuff, unfortunately this doesn't translate into good lectures. I honestly was lost for most of the material as she goes straight into complex mathematics with mediocre explanation. Luckily, her grading scheme is generous and getting a good grade is more than doable. However, I felt like I learned nothing.

COGS 189: This professor is incredibly disorganized. She decides last minute that she wants to incorporate quizzes every week (not even giving us a weeks notice). The Canvas page is difficult to navigate. She expects students to know information beforehand and I'm lost. I'm sure she's great person outside of class but not as a professor. AVOID!

Choose her if you are self-motivated and really wanna LEARN SOMETHING (especially the math principles behind algorithms), and possess a solid foundation in math. If you're just looking for an easy credit, this course is not for you. Math never lie:)

She is a nice lady though her teaching abilities are lacking.  Her grading style is awesome, but the tests are hard anyways.  The material sucks and she really doesn't explain it that well, making homework difficult.  Books don't help and half the stuff they just say "google it."  Frustrating material and could have been explained better!

de Sa is awesome.  My secret favorite part of her class is when she tabs around on her computer and every gets to see like 50+ open windows.  Her teaching style tends to be a mixture of 60% slides and 40% board.  And her homeworks are sometimes cryptic and confusing, however I've never had a problem with the grading.  All and all shes worth it.

analysis.name: Maya VanderSchuit
analysis.verdict: Below average reviews — check if this fits your learning style. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 6 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Her homework assignments are easy and could be done in less than an hour and is complete/incomplete grading. Attendance is mandatory and only get to miss one week of lectures. Participation matters. Her grading for the big projects is outrageous though. If it doesnt fit her ideas then it gets a bad grade.

All CAT classes are mandatory. Readings are relatively important. Must participate in many class discussions. Do NOT slack off on RUBRIC GRADED assignments, as grading is relatively strict. Professor was kind and passionate but the class is not exactly one you can put on the backburner. Final project in Fall 2024 was a 5-10 min. class presentation.

She assigns too many assignments a week and keeps changing the due dates, so if you miss one, your grade will drop like crazy. Also, she doesn't take late work or extra credit. Be ready to submit five assignments a week with this class.

Group assignments are big in this class, but they are just about understanding the readings and writing group reflections about them. Her heavy art influence makes it easy to give ambiguous and broad answers that are interpretive. This class is not hard, and there's support, but you have to show up because attendance matters.

Kind professor but the class itself it boring but easy. Grades on essays did not make sense, but she has an art degree and not much experience grading writing assignments. Lots of participating with classmates in discussions, weekly readings, presentation project at the end of the quarter.

analysis.verdict: Below average reviews — check if this fits your learning style. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 22 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: he's really not as bad as the other comments say! just do the work and show up to class. I would recommend going to office hours or talking to him before/after class bc his perception of u seems to affect his grading a little haha... he's passionate and rambles a lot but is informative most of the time so don't be afraid to take his class!

I was a little nervous about taking this class because of the bad reviews, but honestly, he's not that bad, and has chilled out a lot compared to the past reviews. I like how there are a lot of drafts that he gives feedback on and peer reviews before submitting final drafts. The class itself is a little boring but pretty easy.

Although I saw his initial score (1.7 yikes!!), his class was very doable when I took it. My friend, who says she struggles a lot with writing, also got an A+ in the class. He is very fun to talk to, and don't be afraid to ask questions.

Everyone claims him to be rlly bad but he is actually pretty good. I personally don't love writing but I didn't struggle as much as I thought I would. Class gets pretty boring at times but nothing too bad. I took him because it was the only one available and I honestly don't regret it. I would take him again.

He gives absolutely no feedback. He is a nice person, I had some family issues and was not able to attend class and he still passed me. He overall is not a great professor, he is super unprofessional and the most of my feedback came from my peers. Overall an easy class, just be present and turn in your work on time.
